title: agent-runtime: background process gaps - state transitions, signals, backpressure
summary: Issue #593 tracks staged agent-runtime background process lifecycle hardening: explicit lifecycle state transitions, graceful signal forwarding, bounded stream retention/backpressure metadata, dependency ordering, lifecycle hooks, and pause/resume semantics. Current code already has a BackgroundProcessRegistry, ExecutionPolicy integration, output byte caps, and focused tests, so implementation should extend existing runtime surfaces rather than introducing a competing process manager.

triageFindings: Issue #593 reports missing paused/resumed/zombie lifecycle states, no signal forwarding to process groups, no stream backpressure, no process dependencies, no lifecycle hooks, and no session pause/resume semantics.

The issue comment identifies packages/agent-runtime/src/backgroundProcessRegistry.ts as the primary background process registry and daemon lifecycle/loop/local executor surfaces as adjacent lifecycle areas.

Current staging code already has packages/agent-runtime/src/__tests__/backgroundProcessRegistry.test.ts and partial stdout/stderr output caps through maxOutputBytes, stdoutTruncated, and stderrTruncated.
Current cancel(), killAll(), timeout, and LocalExecutor.destroy still rely on direct child kill semantics and do not expose a shared graceful process-group signal policy.
Current daemon loop queue is in-memory and concurrency-limited, but it has no dependency graph, hook contract, or pause/resume lifecycle model.

relatedIssues.reason: Adjacent agent-runtime crash recovery and durable queue gaps; do not absorb broad restart/watchdog scope unless required.
ExecutionPolicy and sandbox/resource work already touched BackgroundProcessRegistry; reuse policy seams and avoid duplicating sandbox scope.
Observability may consume lifecycle metadata but metrics/export work remains separate.
Adjacent runtime gap issue noted in triage; inspect only for scope boundaries.
Background registry/shell deduplication is related but not duplicate; preserve canonical runtime ownership assumptions.

knownCurrentState.backgroundRegistry: packages/agent-runtime/src/backgroundProcessRegistry.ts owns BackgroundProcessRegistry with spawn(), get(), list(), cancel(), killAll(), dispose(), onComplete callbacks, maxConcurrent, injectable spawnFn, ExecutionPolicy env/cwd validation, timeout handling, and maxOutputBytes retention caps.
knownCurrentState.streamBackpressure: stdoutChunks and stderrChunks are still Buffer arrays, but appendCapped enforces retained bytes when maxOutputBytes is provided and exposes stdoutTruncated/stderrTruncated booleans. Missing: retained/dropped byte counters and explicit completion parity for richer backpressure accounting.
knownCurrentState.stateModel: BackgroundTaskRecord.status currently models running, completed, exited, cancelled, and killed. Missing: timeout-specific status, pause/resume capability states, stale/zombie/orphan diagnostics, and a documented state machine.
knownCurrentState.signalModel: BackgroundProcessRegistry.cancel(), killAll(), and timeout call child.kill('SIGTERM') on the tracked child only. LocalExecutor.destroy adds a direct-child SIGKILL escalation after 3 seconds. There is no shared process-group-aware graceful termination contract.
knownCurrentState.daemonLifecycle: daemon/lifecycle.ts stops the daemon process with SIGTERM then SIGKILL but does not coordinate background children. daemon/loop.ts has activeRuns and queue in memory, waits active runs on abort, and has no dependency graph or lifecycle hooks.
knownCurrentState.tests: packages/agent-runtime/src/__tests__/backgroundProcessRegistry.test.ts currently covers environment policy and output caps. Expand this suite before adding broad new integration tests.

existingInfrastructureToPrefer: existing Vitest tests under packages/agent-runtime/src/__tests__ and execution/resource test directories
reuseAuditSeed.newInfrastructureConstraint: Do not create a second background process manager. Extend the canonical agent-runtime registry and adjacent daemon/executor contracts only where live call paths require it.

acceptanceFocus: Runtime call paths are traced and recorded before implementation begins.
Regression tests are added before production changes and initially fail for missing lifecycle behavior, not syntax or fixture errors.
Stream retention is bounded and exposes explicit per-stream retained/truncated/dropped metadata in snapshots and completion events.

Signal forwarding supports process groups where the platform allows it, preserves direct-child fallback behavior, applies grace timeout and escalation semantics, and remains idempotent across cancel, killAll, timeout, and dispose.
Lifecycle states and transitions are explicit, documented, and tested, including timeout-specific behavior and pause/resume capability gating.
Pause/resume is kept distinct from daemon start/stop unless the implementation deliberately links them with tests and docs.
Dependency ordering has deterministic execution, cycle detection, and explicit dependent failure/skipped behavior if exposed by the implemented API.
Lifecycle hooks have pre/post spawn and pre/post destroy coverage with hook timeouts and explicit failure policy if exposed by the implemented API.
Docs/agent-layer-gaps.md and README claims are updated only for gaps actually closed.
The implementation avoids broad crash recovery, sandboxing, observability export, and cross-package deduplication work unless a maintainer approves scope expansion.
nonGoals: Do not implement full daemon crash recovery, watchdog restart, or durable queue persistence from adjacent runtime issues.
Do not redesign execution sandbox policy beyond reusing existing ExecutionPolicy/resource seams.
Do not implement observability exporters, metrics backends, or distributed tracing in this issue.
Do not move background registry ownership across packages or repeat issue #600 cross-package deduplication work.
Do not silently drop logs without exposed metadata.
Do not require Docker, SSH, Kubernetes, root privileges, or a real process tree for the core unit-test gate.

plannedPhases.name: Issue context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read issue #593, comments, labels, docs/agent-layer-gaps.md, and related issues enough to lock source-of-truth scope.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Search existing background registry, ExecutionPolicy, resource, daemon, executor, and test infrastructure before introducing any new lifecycle API.

plannedPhases.name: Runtime call-path trace
plannedPhases.purpose: Trace run_in_background entry points, registry lifecycle, stream listeners, cancellation, timeout, daemon shutdown, queue handling, exports, and public type surfaces.

plannedPhases.name: Lifecycle contract design
plannedPhases.purpose: Design explicit stream, signal, state-machine, pause/resume, dependency, and hook semantics with platform capability handling.

plannedPhases.name: Regression coverage
plannedPhases.purpose: Add focused tests first for bounded streams, signal escalation, lifecycle states, pause/resume capability gates, dependency ordering, and hooks.

plannedPhases.name: Design review
plannedPhases.purpose: Challenge API compatibility, platform semantics, hook/dependency failure behavior, and scope boundaries before implementation.

plannedPhases.name: Incremental implementation
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement stream metadata, graceful termination, state transitions, pause/resume, dependencies, hooks, and docs in staged attempts.

plannedPhases.name: Verification loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Run targeted tests, package tests/builds, metadata checks, and iterate up to three attempts when verification or review fails.

plannedPhases.name: Compatibility review
plannedPhases.purpose: Review public API changes, race conditions, signal behavior, bounded memory behavior, hook/dependency failure policy, and docs consistency.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Compare issue requirements directly to implementation, verification, and review artifacts before declaring completion.

breakpoints.when: Only if design or implementation discovers a real maintainer decision, such as making new lifecycle states public, choosing unsupported-platform behavior for pause/resume, or expanding scope into daemon crash recovery/durable queues.
Only if final acceptance finds a partially implemented gap that requires explicit deferral or scope approval.

qualityGates: Phase 0 reuse-audit findings are rendered before implementation recommendations.
runtimeCallPaths are recorded in process output and cover BackgroundProcessRegistry, LocalExecutor.destroy, daemon lifecycle, and daemon loop queue handling.
Tests are authored before production implementation and the red phase is verified for expected missing behavior.
Stream tests prove bounded retention and surfaced metadata for stdout and stderr snapshots plus completion events.
Signal tests prove process-group target or fallback target, grace timeout, escalation, and idempotency without hanging.
State-machine tests prove valid transitions for running, completed, exited, cancelled, killed, timed out, pause/resume capability behavior, and stale/zombie diagnostics where implemented.
Dependency and lifecycle hook tests prove ordering, cycle detection, timeout, failure policy, and no hidden deadlock where those APIs are introduced.
Compatibility review approves public API changes and platform-specific behavior.
Final spec guard confirms no unapproved scope expansion into crash recovery, sandboxing, observability, or cross-package deduplication.

processLibraryResearch.shapeSelected: Flat phase list because the issue is a known agent-runtime feature/architecture bundle with identifiable surfaces rather than an unknown-cause forensic bug.
processLibraryResearch.repoPolicyApplied: Use agent tasks rather than kind:shell tasks; verification agents must run commands directly and report exact command outputs.
